wins: iter-2 (+10% headline): PDL split→reduce via gdc_launch_dependents/gdc_wait + launch_pdl=True; hides ~2us reduce-launch tail
iter-3,4,6 (+20% B=1): SPLIT=1 fused path + deep pipe (num_stages=6 fused; 1 CTA/SM at B=1 → 228KB SMEM headroom)
iter-12,13 (+6% B=64, dispatched): grid (H_KV,SPLIT,B) — h_kv at pid(0) for B=64; NCU DRAM 51.8→56.85%
iter-14 (+1.3%): reduce kernel grid swap (B,H_QO)→(H_QO,B) — adjacent reduce CTAs share (b,h_kv) for L1/L2 hits on partial
dead_ends: iter-1 (-10-15% uniform): one-launch atomic-last-writer reduce — Triton can't keep dead branch's reduce-locals out of split path's register live-set; per-CTA atomic_add forces membar.gl flush on every CTA exit. Don't retry without restructuring (persistent reducer-CTA pool).
iter-5 (-2.86%): split num_stages 4→5 — SMEM pressure / L2 contention at edge. Round-1's per-stage win stopped at 4.
iter-7 (-0.38% noise): fused num_stages 6→8 — already saturated HBM-latency hiding at 6 stages.
iter-8 (-3.20%): B=16 SPLIT 4→8 even under PDL — chunks too short to amortize prefetch (round-1 verdict reconfirmed).
iter-9 (-2.35%): split num_warps 4→2 uniform — opposite-sign effects on different batches (B=16 small gain / B=64 -0.148); dispatch per-batch in iter-10 also a wash.
iter-11 (-0.06% true no-op): grid (B,H_KV,S)→(B,S,H_KV) without moving pid(0) binding — Triton's HW dispatch is pid(0)-fastest, so swapping later dims is a no-op.
iter-12 applied to all batches (-7% B=16): h_kv-fastest grid HURTS B=16 — its working set already fits L2 (16MB ≪ 192MB); dispatch by batch in iter-13.
ncu_profile: split kernel B=64 (post-iter13 dispatch): DRAM 56.85% (was 51.8% at round-1), achieved occupancy 29.5% of theoretical 31.25% (92 reg/thread cap), Est. Local Speedup 68.75% if occupancy maxed. Still memory-bound.
